attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway specialist who attacks from front groups on penultimate climbs, then time-trials solo to finish. Prefers mountain-top finishes where he can destroy breakaway companions 10-15km from finish and sustain power advantage alone.
team_tactics: Operates with tactical freedom in breakaways, teams often allow him significant leeway (5+ minutes despite close GC gaps). Not reliant on domestique support; thrives when sent up the road early to manage own efforts.
terrain_preference: Excels on sustained mountain-top finishes where consistent power trumps explosiveness. Strong on technical descents (Splugen example). Weather-shortened or late-race fatigue stages neutralize explosive rivals and favor his steady diesel engine.
vulnerability: Struggles on explosive final climbs when fresh (dropped early on steep MTFs). At age 35+, form consistency between races uncertain. Cannot respond when isolated and attacked repeatedly by pure climbers.
key_patterns: Destroys breakaway companions on penultimate climb, then solos final 10-15km (3 observations)
GC teams force to chase despite close GC gaps, respecting his threat (3 observations)
Perfect energy management: holds minimal advantages through superior pacing, not power (4 observations)
Late-career breakthrough: first GT wins at 33-35 years old when fatigue levels field (3 observations)
Tactically alert opportunist: exploits coverage gaps and intermediate sprints for GC seconds (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: Showed GC form at age 35 in 2023 Vuelta (teams respected 23sec gap), but questioned whether purely opportunistic. No dominant performances like 2021 peak victories.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 9
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 9
